idioms.name: use_context_managers
idioms.description: Use 'with' statements for all resources that support context managers — files, locks, connections, sessions.
idioms.detection: Detect file open() without with statement. Detect manual close() calls. Detect try/finally for resource cleanup.
idioms.fix: Wrap in with statement. Implement __enter__/__exit__ for custom resource types.
idioms.bad_example:
f = open('data.txt')
try:
    data = f.read()
finally:
    f.close()
idioms.good_example:
with open('data.txt') as f:
    data = f.read()

idioms.name: use_type_hints
idioms.description: Add type hints to all function signatures and class attributes. Use modern syntax (X | None instead of Optional[X]).
idioms.detection: Detect functions without type annotations. Detect use of typing.Optional instead of X | None (3.10+).
idioms.fix: Add type hints to parameters and return types. Use built-in generics (list[int] not List[int]).
idioms.bad_example:
def process(items, threshold):
    return [i for i in items if i > threshold]
idioms.good_example:
def process(items: list[float], threshold: float) -> list[float]:
    return [i for i in items if i > threshold]

idioms.name: use_dataclasses
idioms.description: Use @dataclass for data containers instead of plain classes with manual __init__. Use frozen=True for immutable data.
idioms.detection: Detect classes whose __init__ only assigns self.x = x for multiple attributes.
idioms.fix: Convert to @dataclass. Use frozen=True for value objects.

idioms.name: no_mutable_defaults
idioms.description: Never use mutable default arguments (list, dict, set). They persist across calls.
idioms.detection: Detect def f(x=[]) or def f(x={}) or def f(x=set()) patterns.
idioms.fix: Use None as default and create fresh mutable inside the function.

idioms.name: use_pathlib
idioms.description: Use pathlib.Path instead of os.path for file path manipulation.
idioms.fix: Replace with Path() operations: Path(a) / b, path.exists(), path.parent.
idioms.good_example:
from pathlib import Path
path = Path(base) / 'data' / 'file.txt'

idioms.name: use_generators
idioms.description: Use generators and iterators for large sequences instead of building entire lists in memory.
idioms.detection: Detect functions that build large lists and return them when yielding would work.
idioms.fix: Replace return with yield. Use generator expressions instead of list comprehensions for large data.

idioms.name: use_properties
idioms.description: Use @property instead of Java-style getX()/setX() methods.
idioms.detection: Detect get_* / set_* method pairs that access private attributes.
